title: Incorrect Flip of Trailing Zeros in Binary Representation

statement.markdown:
Given an integer n, count the number of trailing zeros in the binary representation of n. The function should return the count of trailing zeros.

Example:
Input: n = 18
Output: 1
Explanation: Binary representation of 18 is 10010 which has one trailing zero.
statement.outputFormat: Return the count of trailing zeros in the binary representation of n
statement.notes: You can assume n will always be non-negative

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: Count the number of trailing zeros by dividing the number by 2 until it is not divisible by 2
editorial.bruteForce: Try using a for loop to check each bit of the number
editorial.optimalSolution: Check if the number is even or odd and remove the least significant bit if even
